text.title: Success Metrics Definition Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Success Metrics Definition application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Spec MUST cite exactly ONE north-star metric linked to a business outcome.
rule.rationale: Multi-north-stars dilute focus and create conflicting incentives.

rule.statement: Supporting KPIs MUST be ≤5 and partitioned across AARRR (max 1 per stage).
rule.rationale: More than 5 KPIs nobody reads; AARRR partition ensures funnel coverage.

rule.statement: Vanity metrics (pageviews, raw signups without activation) MUST be explicitly listed as excluded.
rule.rationale: Vanity exclusions document the discipline; un-flagged vanity re-creeps.

rule.statement: Every KPI MUST carry baseline + target + measurement window.
rule.rationale: Targets without baselines are wishes; targets without windows are unbounded.

rule.statement: Every KPI MUST have an instrumentation status; un-instrumented KPIs park-listed.
rule.rationale: Un-instrumented KPIs become guesswork that erodes trust in the dashboard.
